mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances following rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances following the trigger, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, positive industrial production growth signals resilient manufacturing activity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) specifically during tightening regimes, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of positive growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index readings above 215 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >50% of months following the threshold breach, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for lagged market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting earnings forecasts and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with statistical significance (p > 0.1) in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate profit margins and revenue expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across rolling windows, or returns are negative in >55% of high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below 108.0 indicates weakening manufacturing demand and potential inventory gluts, prompting equity sell-offs over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances following the threshold breach, or the signal shows no incremental predictive value over a simple moving average.
